title: KDPE: A Kernel Density Estimation Strategy for Diffusion Policy Trajectory Selection
title_zh: KDPE：一种用于扩散策略轨迹选择的核密度估计策略
abstract:
Learning robot policies that capture multimodality in the training data has been a long-standing open challenge for behavior cloning. Recent approaches tackle the problem by modeling the conditional action distribution with generative models. One of these approaches is Diffusion Policy, which relies on a diffusion model to denoise random points into robot action trajectories. While achieving state-of-the-art performance, it has two main drawbacks that may lead the robot out of the data distribution during policy execution. First, the stochasticity of the denoising process can highly impact on the quality of generated trajectory of actions. Second, being a supervised learning approach, it can learn data outliers from the dataset used for training. Recent work focuses on mitigating these limitations by combining Diffusion Policy either with large-scale training or with classical behavior cloning algorithms. Instead, we propose KDPE, a Kernel Density Estimation-based strategy that filters out potentially harmful trajectories output of Diffusion Policy while keeping a low test-time computational overhead.
For Kernel Density Estimation, we propose a manifold-aware kernel to model a probability density function for actions composed of end-effector Cartesian position, orientation, and gripper state. KDPE overall achieves better performance than Diffusion Policy on simulated single-arm RoboMimic and MimicGen tasks, and on three real robot experiments:PickPlush, a tabletop grasping task, CubeSort, a multimodal pick and place task, and CoffeeMaking, a task that requires long-horizon capabilities and precise execution.

The code will be released upon acceptance and additional material is provided on our anonymized project page:https://kdpe-robotics.github.io.
abstract_zh: 学习能够捕捉训练数据中多模态性的机器人策略一直是行为克隆中长期存在的开放挑战。最近的方法通过使用生成模型对条件动作分布进行建模来解决该问题。其中一种方法是扩散策略，它依赖扩散模型将随机点去噪为机器人动作轨迹。尽管取得了最先进的性能，但它有两个主要缺点，可能导致策略执行期间机器人偏离数据分布。首先，去噪过程的随机性会严重影响生成动作轨迹的质量。其次，作为一种监督学习方法，它可能从训练数据集中学习到数据异常值。最近的研究通过将扩散策略与大规模训练或经典行为克隆算法相结合来缓解这些限制。相反，我们提出了KDPE，一种基于核密度估计的策略，用于过滤扩散策略输出的潜在有害轨迹，同时保持较低的测试时计算开销。对于核密度估计，我们提出了一种流形感知核，用于建模由末端执行器笛卡尔位置、方向和夹爪状态组成的动作的概率密度函数。KDPE在模拟单臂RoboMimic和MimicGen任务以及三个真实机器人实验（PickPlush桌面抓取任务、CubeSort多模态拾取和放置任务以及CoffeeMaking需要长时域能力和精确执行的任务）上整体优于扩散策略。代码将在接收后发布，更多材料请访问我们的匿名项目页面：https://kdpe-robotics.github.io。
tldr_zh: 提出KDPE，用核密度估计过滤扩散策略的异常轨迹，提升性能且计算开销低。
cover_zh: 扩散策略总跑偏？KDPE用密度估计一招过滤异常轨迹，性能飙升！
